001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.classic.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.PredicateUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
024
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027import java.util.stream.*;
028
029import org.apache.http.*;
030import org.apache.http.Header;
031import org.apache.http.util.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders;
036
037/**
038 * A simple list of HTTP headers with various convenience methods.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
041 * <p class='bjava'>
042 *    HeaderList <jv>headers</jv> = HeaderList
043 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
044 *       .append(Accept.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"text/xml"</js>))
045 *       .append(<js>"Content-Type"</js>, ()-&gt;<jsm>getDynamicContentTypeFromSomewhere</jsm>());
046 * </p>
047 *
048 * <p>
049 * Convenience creators are provided for creating lists with minimal code:
050 * <p class='bjava'>
051 *    HeaderList <jv>headers</jv> = HeaderList.<jsm>of</jsm>(Accept.<jsf>TEXT_XML</jsf>, ContentType.<jsf>TEXT_XML</jsf>);
052 * </p>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * Static methods are provided on {@link HttpHeaders} to further simplify creation of header lists.
056 * <p class='bjava'>
057 *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*;
058 *
059 *    HeaderList <jv>headers</jv> = <jsm>headerList</jsm>(<jsm>accept</jsm>(<js>"text/xml"</js>), <jsm>contentType</jsm>(<js>"text/xml"</js>));
060 * </p>
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestCommon">juneau-rest-common Basics</a>
064 * </ul>
065 */
066@SuppressWarnings({
067   "java:S110",  // Inheritance depth acceptable for this class hierarchy
068   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
069   "java:S2160"  // equals() inherited from AbstractList; list equality is element-based, which is correct for header lists
070})
071public class HeaderList extends ControlledArrayList<Header> {
072
073   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
074   private static final String ARG_type = "type";
075
076   /** Represents no header list in annotations. */
077   public static final class Void extends HeaderList {
078      private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
079   }
080
081   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
082
083   /**
084    * Instantiates a new list.
085    *
086    * @return A new list.
087    */
088   public static HeaderList create() {
089      return new HeaderList();
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Creates a new {@link HeaderList} initialized with the specified headers.
094    *
095    * @param headers
096    *    The headers to add to the list.
097    *    <br><jk>null</jk> entries are ignored.
098    * @return A new unmodifiable instance, never <jk>null</jk>.
099    */
100   public static HeaderList of(Header...headers) {
101      return new HeaderList().append(headers);
102   }
103
104   /**
105    * Creates a new {@link HeaderList} initialized with the specified headers.
106    *
107    * @param headers
108    *    The headers to add to the list.
109    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
110    *    <br><jk>null</jk> entries are ignored.
111    * @return A new unmodifiable instance, never <jk>null</jk>.
112    */
113   public static HeaderList of(List<Header> headers) {
114      return new HeaderList().append(headers);
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Creates a new {@link HeaderList} initialized with the specified name/value pairs.
119    *
120    * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
121    * <p class='bjava'>
122    *    HeaderList <jv>headers</jv> = HeaderList.<jsm>ofPairs</jsm>(<js>"Accept"</js>, <js>"text/xml"</js>, <js>"Content-Type"</js>, <js>"text/xml"</js>);
123    * </p>
124    *
125    * @param pairs
126    *    Initial list of pairs.
127    *    <br>Must be an even number of parameters representing key/value pairs.
128    * @throws RuntimeException If odd number of parameters were specified.
129    * @return A new instance.
130    */
131   public static HeaderList ofPairs(String...pairs) {
132      var x = new HeaderList();
133      if (pairs == null)
134         pairs = new String[0];
135      assertArg(pairs.length % 2 == 0, "Odd number of parameters passed into HeaderList.ofPairs()");
136      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
137         x.add(BasicHeader.of(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]));
138      return x;
139   }
140
141   private transient VarResolver varResolver;
142   boolean caseSensitive;
143
144   /**
145    * Constructor.
146    */
147   public HeaderList() {
148      super(false);
149   }
150
151   /**
152    * Copy constructor.
153    *
154    * @param copyFrom The bean to copy.
155    */
156   protected HeaderList(HeaderList copyFrom) {
157      super(false, copyFrom);
158      caseSensitive = copyFrom.caseSensitive;
159   }
160
161   /**
162    * Adds the specified header to the end of the headers in this list.
163    *
164    * @param value The header to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
165    * @return This object.
166    */
167   public HeaderList append(Header value) {
168      if (nn(value))
169         add(value);
170      return this;
171   }
172
173   /**
174    * Adds the specified headers to the end of the headers in this list.
175    *
176    * @param values The headers to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
177    * @return This object.
178    */
179   public HeaderList append(Header...values) {
180      if (nn(values))
181         for (var value : values)
182            if (nn(value))
183               append(value);
184      return this;
185   }
186
187   /**
188    * Adds the specified headers to the end of the headers in this list.
189    *
190    * @param values The headers to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
191    * @return This object.
192    */
193   public HeaderList append(List<Header> values) {
194      if (nn(values))
195         values.forEach(this::append);
196      return this;
197   }
198
199   /**
200    * Appends the specified header to the end of this list.
201    *
202    * <p>
203    * The header is added as a {@link BasicHeader}.
204    *
205    * @param name The header name.
206    * @param value The header value.
207    * @return This object.
208    */
209   public HeaderList append(String name, Object value) {
210      return append(createPart(name, value));
211   }
212
213   /**
214    * Appends the specified header to the end of this list using a value supplier.
215    *
216    * <p>
217    * The header is added as a {@link BasicHeader}.
218    *
219    * <p>
220    * Value is re-evaluated on each call to {@link BasicHeader#getValue()}.
221    *
222    * @param name The header name.
223    * @param value The header value supplier.
224    * @return This object.
225    */
226   public HeaderList append(String name, Supplier<?> value) {
227      return append(createPart(name, value));
228   }
229
230   /**
231    * Specifies that the headers in this list should be treated as case-sensitive.
232    *
233    * <p>
234    * The default behavior is case-insensitive.
235    *
236    * @param value The new value for this setting.
237    * @return This object.
238    */
239   public HeaderList caseSensitive(boolean value) {
240      assertModifiable();
241      caseSensitive = value;
242      return this;
243   }
244
245   /**
246    * Tests if headers with the given name are contained within this list.
247    *
248    * <p>
249    * Header name comparison is case insensitive.
250    *
251    * @param name The header name.
252    * @return <jk>true</jk> if at least one header with the name is present.
253    */
254   public boolean contains(String name) {
255      return stream().anyMatch(x -> eq(x.getName(), name));
256   }
257
258   /**
259    * Makes a copy of this list.
260    *
261    * @return A new copy of this list.
262    */
263   public HeaderList copy() {
264      return new HeaderList(this);
265   }
266
267   /**
268    * Performs an action on all matching headers in this list.
269    *
270    * <p>
271    * This is the preferred method for iterating over headers as it does not involve
272    * creation or copy of lists/arrays.
273    *
274    * @param filter A predicate to apply to each element to determine if it should be included.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
275    * @param action An action to perform on each element.
276    * @return This object.
277    */
278   public HeaderList forEach(Predicate<Header> filter, Consumer<Header> action) {
279      forEach(x -> consumeIf(filter, action, x));
280      return this;
281   }
282
283   /**
284    * Performs an action on all headers with the specified name in this list.
285    *
286    * <p>
287    * This is the preferred method for iterating over headers as it does not involve
288    * creation or copy of lists/arrays.
289    *
290    * @param name The header name.
291    * @param action An action to perform on each element.
292    * @return This object.
293    */
294   public HeaderList forEach(String name, Consumer<Header> action) {
295      return forEach(x -> eq(name, x.getName()), action);
296   }
297
298   /**
299    * Performs an action on the values for all matching headers in this list.
300    *
301    * @param filter A predicate to apply to each element to determine if it should be included.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
302    * @param action An action to perform on each element.
303    * @return This object.
304    */
305   public HeaderList forEachValue(Predicate<Header> filter, Consumer<String> action) {
306      return forEach(filter, x -> action.accept(x.getValue()));
307   }
308
309   /**
310    * Performs an action on the values of all matching headers in this list.
311    *
312    * @param name The header name.
313    * @param action An action to perform on each element.
314    * @return This object.
315    */
316   public HeaderList forEachValue(String name, Consumer<String> action) {
317      return forEach(name, x -> action.accept(x.getValue()));
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * Gets a header representing all of the header values with the given name.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Same as {@link #get(String, Class)} but the header name is pulled from the {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Header#name()} or
325    *    {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Header#value()} annotations.
326    *
327    * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
328    * <p class='bjava'>
329    *    Age <jv>age</jv> = headerList.get(Age.<jk>class</jk>);
330    * </p>
331    *
332    * @param <T> The return type.
333    * @param type The header implementation class.
334    * @return A header with a condensed value or <jk>null</jk> if no headers by the given name are present
335    */
336   public <T> Optional<T> get(Class<T> type) {
337      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
338
339      String name = HeaderBeanMeta.of(type).getSchema().getName();
340      assertArg(nn(name), "Header name could not be found on bean type ''{0}''", cn(type));
341
342      return get(name, type);
343   }
344
345   /**
346    * Gets a header representing all of the header values with the given name.
347    *
348    * <p>
349    * If more that one header with the given name exists the values will be combined with <js>", "</js> as per
350    * <a href='https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2616#section-4.2'>RFC 2616 Section 4.2</a>.
351    *
352    * @param name The header name.
353    * @return A header with a condensed value, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no headers by the given name are present
354    */
355   public Optional<Header> get(String name) {
356
357      Header first = null;
358      List<Header> rest = null;
359      for (var x : this) {
360         if (eq(x.getName(), name)) {
361            if (first == null)
362               first = x;
363            else {
364               if (rest == null)
365                  rest = list();
366               rest.add(x);
367            }
368         }
369      }
370
371      if (first == null)
372         return oe();
373
374      if (rest == null)
375         return o(first);
376
377      var sb = new CharArrayBuffer(128);
378      sb.append(first.getValue());
379      for (var element : rest) {
380         sb.append(", ");
381         sb.append(element.getValue());
382      }
383
384      return o(new BasicHeader(name, sb.toString()));
385   }
386
387   /**
388    * Gets a header representing all of the header values with the given name.
389    *
390    * <p>
391    * If more that one header with the given name exists the values will be combined with <js>", "</js> as per
392    * <a href='https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2616#section-4.2'>RFC 2616 Section 4.2</a>.
393    *
394    * <p>
395    * The implementation class must have a public constructor taking in one of the following argument lists:
396    * <ul>
397    *    <li><c>X(String <jv>value</jv>)</c>
398    *    <li><c>X(Object <jv>value</jv>)</c>
399    *    <li><c>X(String <jv>name</jv>, String <jv>value</jv>)</c>
400    *    <li><c>X(String <jv>name</jv>, Object <jv>value</jv>)</c>
401    * </ul>
402    *
403    * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
404    * <p class='bjava'>
405    *    BasicIntegerHeader <jv>age</jv> = headerList.get(<js>"Age"</js>, BasicIntegerHeader.<jk>class</jk>);
406    * </p>
407    *
408    * @param <T> The header implementation class.
409    * @param name The header name.
410    * @param type The header implementation class.
411    * @return A header with a condensed value or <jk>null</jk> if no headers by the given name are present
412    */
413   public <T> Optional<T> get(String name, Class<T> type) {
414
415      Header first = null;
416      List<Header> rest = null;
417      for (var x : this) {
418         if (eq(x.getName(), name)) {
419            if (first == null)
420               first = x;
421            else {
422               if (rest == null)
423                  rest = list();
424               rest.add(x);
425            }
426         }
427      }
428
429      if (first == null)
430         return oe();
431
432      if (rest == null)
433         return o(HeaderBeanMeta.of(type).construct(name, first.getValue()));
434
435      var sb = new CharArrayBuffer(128);
436      sb.append(first.getValue());
437      for (var element : rest) {
438         sb.append(", ");
439         sb.append(element.getValue());
440      }
441
442      return o(HeaderBeanMeta.of(type).construct(name, sb.toString()));
443   }
444
445   /**
446    * Gets all of the headers.
447    *
448    * <p>
449    * The returned array maintains the relative order in which the headers were added.
450    * Each call creates a new array not backed by this list.
451    *
452    * <p>
453    * As a general rule, it's more efficient to use the other methods with consumers to
454    * get headers.
455    *
456    * @return An array containing all headers, never <jk>null</jk>.
457    */
458   public Header[] getAll() { return stream().toArray(Header[]::new); }
459
460   /**
461    * Gets all of the headers with the given name.
462    *
463    * <p>
464    * The returned array maintains the relative order in which the headers were added.
465    * Header name comparison is case insensitive.
466    * Headers with null values are ignored.
467    * Each call creates a new array not backed by this list.
468    *
469    * <p>
470    * As a general rule, it's more efficient to use the other methods with consumers to
471    * get headers.
472    *
473    * @param name The header name.
474    *
475    * @return An array containing all matching headers, never <jk>null</jk>.
476    */
477   public Header[] getAll(String name) {
478      return stream().filter(x -> eq(x.getName(), name)).toArray(Header[]::new);
479   }
480
481   /**
482    * Gets the first header with the given name.
483    *
484    * <p>
485    * Header name comparison is case insensitive.
486    *
487    * @param name The header name.
488    * @return The first matching header, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found.
489    */
490   public Optional<Header> getFirst(String name) {
491      for (var i = 0; i < size(); i++) {
492         var x = get(i);
493         if (eq(x.getName(), name))
494            return o(x);
495      }
496      return oe();
497   }
498
499   /**
500    * Gets the last header with the given name.
501    *
502    * <p>
503    * Header name comparison is case insensitive.
504    *
505    * @param name The header name.
506    * @return The last matching header, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found.
507    */
508   public Optional<Header> getLast(String name) {
509      for (var i = size() - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
510         var x = get(i);
511         if (eq(x.getName(), name))
512            return o(x);
513      }
514      return oe();
515   }
516
517   /**
518    * Returns all the string values for all headers with the specified name.
519    *
520    * @param name The header name.
521    * @return An array containing all values.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
522    */
523   public String[] getValues(String name) {
524      return stream().filter(x -> eq(x.getName(), name)).map(Header::getValue).toArray(String[]::new);
525   }
526
527   /**
528    * Returns an iterator over this list of headers.
529    *
530    * @return A new iterator over this list of headers.
531    */
532   public HeaderIterator headerIterator() {
533      return new BasicHeaderIterator(toArray(new Header[0]), null, caseSensitive);
534   }
535
536   /**
537    * Returns an iterator over the headers with a given name in this list.
538    *
539    * @param name The name of the headers over which to iterate, or <jk>null</jk> for all headers
540    *
541    * @return A new iterator over the matching headers in this list.
542    */
543   public HeaderIterator headerIterator(String name) {
544      return new BasicHeaderIterator(getAll(name), name, caseSensitive);
545   }
546
547   /**
548    * Adds the specified header to the beginning of the headers in this list.
549    *
550    * @param value The header to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
551    * @return This object.
552    */
553   public HeaderList prepend(Header value) {
554      if (nn(value))
555         add(0, value);
556      return this;
557   }
558
559   /**
560    * Adds the specified headers to the beginning of the headers in this list.
561    *
562    * @param values The headers to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
563    * @return This object.
564    */
565   public HeaderList prepend(Header...values) {
566      if (nn(values))
567         prepend(l(values));
568      return this;
569   }
570
571   /**
572    * Adds the specified headers to the beginning of the headers in this list.
573    *
574    * @param values The headers to add.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
575    * @return This object.
576    */
577   public HeaderList prepend(List<Header> values) {
578      if (nn(values))
579         addAll(0, values);
580      return this;
581   }
582
583   /**
584    * Appends the specified header to the beginning of this list.
585    *
586    * <p>
587    * The header is added as a {@link BasicHeader}.
588    *
589    * @param name The header name.
590    * @param value The header value.
591    * @return This object.
592    */
593   public HeaderList prepend(String name, Object value) {
594      return prepend(createPart(name, value));
595   }
596
597   /**
598    * Appends the specified header to the beginning of this list using a value supplier.
599    *
600    * <p>
601    * The header is added as a {@link BasicHeader}.
602    *
603    * <p>
604    * Value is re-evaluated on each call to {@link BasicHeader#getValue()}.
605    *
606    * @param name The header name.
607    * @param value The header value supplier.
608    * @return This object.
609    */
610   public HeaderList prepend(String name, Supplier<?> value) {
611      return prepend(createPart(name, value));
612   }
613
614   /**
615    * Removes the specified header from this list.
616    *
617    * @param value The header to remove.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
618    * @return This object.
619    */
620   public HeaderList remove(Header value) {
621      if (nn(value))
622         removeIf(x -> eq(x.getName(), value.getName()) && eq(x.getValue(), value.getValue()));
623      return this;
624   }
625
626   /**
627    * Removes the specified headers from this list.
628    *
629    * @param values The headers to remove.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
630    * @return This object.
631    */
632   public HeaderList remove(Header...values) {
633      for (var value : values)
634         remove(value);
635      return this;
636   }
637
638   /**
639    * Removes the specified headers from this list.
640    *
641    * @param values The headers to remove.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
642    * @return This object.
643    */
644   public HeaderList remove(List<Header> values) {
645      if (nn(values))
646         values.forEach(this::remove);
647      return this;
648   }
649
650   /**
651    * Removes the header with the specified name from this list.
652    *
653    * @param name The header name.
654    * @return This object.
655    */
656   public HeaderList remove(String name) {
657      removeIf(x -> eq(x.getName(), name));
658      return this;
659   }
660
661   /**
662    * Removes the header with the specified name from this list.
663    *
664    * @param names The header name.
665    * @return This object.
666    */
667   public HeaderList remove(String...names) {
668      if (nn(names))
669         for (var name : names)
670            remove(name);
671      return this;
672   }
673
674   /**
675    * Removes all headers from this list.
676    *
677    * @return This object.
678    */
679   public HeaderList removeAll() {
680      clear();
681      return this;
682   }
683
684   /**
685    * Allows header values to contain SVL variables.
686    *
687    * <p>
688    * Resolves variables in header values when using the following methods:
689    * <ul>
690    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #append(String, Object) append(String,Object)}
691    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #append(String, Supplier) append(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
692    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #prepend(String, Object) prepend(String,Object)}
693    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #prepend(String, Supplier) prepend(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
694    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #set(String, Object) set(String,Object)}
695    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #set(String, Supplier) set(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
696    * </ul>
697    *
698    * <p>
699    * Uses {@link VarResolver#DEFAULT} to resolve variables.
700    *
701    * @return This object.
702    */
703   public HeaderList resolving() {
704      return resolving(VarResolver.DEFAULT);
705   }
706
707   /**
708    * Allows header values to contain SVL variables.
709    *
710    * <p>
711    * Resolves variables in header values when using the following methods:
712    * <ul>
713    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #append(String, Object) append(String,Object)}
714    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #append(String, Supplier) append(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
715    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #prepend(String, Object) prepend(String,Object)}
716    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #prepend(String, Supplier) prepend(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
717    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #set(String, Object) set(String,Object)}
718    *    <li class='jm'>{@link #set(String, Supplier) set(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
719    * </ul>
720    *
721    * @param varResolver The variable resolver to use for resolving variables.
722    * @return This object.
723    */
724   public HeaderList resolving(VarResolver varResolver) {
725      assertModifiable();
726      this.varResolver = varResolver;
727      return this;
728   }
729
730   /**
731    * Adds or replaces the header(s) with the same name.
732    *
733    * <p>
734    * If no header with the same name is found the given header is added to the end of the list.
735    *
736    * @param value The headers to replace.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
737    * @return This object.
738    */
739   @SuppressWarnings({
740      "java:S127" // Loop counter j decrements when removing elements
741   })
742   public HeaderList set(Header value) {
743      if (nn(value)) {
744         var replaced = false;
745         for (int i = 0, j = size(); i < j; i++) {
746            var x = get(i);
747            if (eq(x.getName(), value.getName())) {
748               if (replaced) {
749                  remove(i);
750                  j--;
751               } else {
752                  set(i, value);
753                  replaced = true;
754               }
755            }
756         }
757
758         if (! replaced)
759            add(value);
760      }
761
762      return this;
763   }
764
765   /**
766    * Adds or replaces the header(s) with the same name.
767    *
768    * <p>
769    * If no header with the same name is found the given header is added to the end of the list.
770    *
771    * @param values The headers to replace.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
772    * @return This object.
773    */
774   public HeaderList set(Header...values) {
775      if (nn(values))
776         set(l(values));
777      return this;
778   }
779
780   /**
781    * Replaces the first occurrence of the headers with the same name.
782    *
783    * <p>
784    * If no header with the same name is found the given header is added to the end of the list.
785    *
786    * @param values The headers to replace.  <jk>null</jk> values are ignored.
787    * @return This object.
788    */
789   
790   @SuppressWarnings({
791      "java:S127", // S127: loop counter j2 decrements when removing
792      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
793   })
794   public HeaderList set(List<Header> values) {
795
796      if (nn(values)) {
797         for (var h : values) {
798            if (nn(h)) {
799               for (int i2 = 0, j2 = size(); i2 < j2; i2++) {
800                  var x = get(i2);
801                  if (eq(x.getName(), h.getName())) {
802                     remove(i2);
803                     j2--;
804                  }
805               }
806            }
807         }
808
809         for (var x : values) {
810            if (nn(x)) {
811               add(x);
812            }
813         }
814      }
815
816      return this;
817   }
818
819   /**
820    * Replaces the first occurrence of the headers with the same name.
821    *
822    * @param name The header name.
823    * @param value The header value.
824    * @return This object.
825    */
826   public HeaderList set(String name, Object value) {
827      return set(createPart(name, value));
828   }
829
830   /**
831    * Replaces the first occurrence of the headers with the same name.
832    *
833    * @param name The header name.
834    * @param value The header value.
835    * @return This object.
836    */
837   public HeaderList set(String name, Supplier<?> value) {
838      return set(createPart(name, value));
839   }
840
841   /**
842    * Makes a copy of this list of headers and adds a collection of default headers.
843    *
844    * <p>
845    * Default headers are set if they're not already in the list.
846    *
847    * @param headers The list of default headers.
848    * @return A new list, or the same list if the headers were empty.
849    */
850   public HeaderList setDefault(Header...headers) {
851      if (nn(headers))
852         setDefault(l(headers));
853      return this;
854   }
855
856   /**
857    * Adds a collection of default headers.
858    *
859    * <p>
860    * Default headers are set if they're not already in the list.
861    *
862    * @param headers The list of default headers.
863    * @return This object.
864    */
865   public HeaderList setDefault(List<Header> headers) {
866      if (nn(headers))
867         headers.stream().filter(x -> nn(x) && ! contains(x.getName())).forEach(this::set);
868      return this;
869   }
870
871   /**
872    * Replaces the first occurrence of the headers with the same name.
873    *
874    * @param name The header name.
875    * @param value The header value.
876    * @return This object.
877    */
878   public HeaderList setDefault(String name, Object value) {
879      return setDefault(createPart(name, value));
880   }
881
882   /**
883    * Replaces the first occurrence of the headers with the same name.
884    *
885    * @param name The header name.
886    * @param value The header value.
887    * @return This object.
888    */
889   public HeaderList setDefault(String name, Supplier<?> value) {
890      return setDefault(createPart(name, value));
891   }
892
893   @Override /* Overridden from ControlledArrayList */
894   public HeaderList setUnmodifiable() {
895      super.setUnmodifiable();
896      return this;
897   }
898
899   /**
900    * Returns a stream of the headers in this list with the specified name.
901    *
902    * <p>
903    * This does not involve a copy of the underlying array of <c>Header</c> objects so should perform well.
904    *
905    * @param name The header name.
906    * @return This object.
907    */
908   public Stream<Header> stream(String name) {
909      return stream().filter(x -> eq(name, x.getName()));
910   }
911
912   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
913   public String toString() {
914      return "[" + join(this, ", ") + "]";
915   }
916
917   /**
918    * Creates a new header out of the specified name/value pair.
919    *
920    * @param name The header name.
921    * @param value The header value.
922    * @return A new header.
923    */
924   private Header createPart(String name, Object value) {
925      var isResolving = nn(varResolver);
926
927      if (value instanceof Supplier<?> value2) {
928         return isResolving ? new BasicHeader(name, resolver(value2)) : new BasicHeader(name, value2);
929      }
930      return isResolving ? new BasicHeader(name, resolver(value)) : new BasicHeader(name, value);
931   }
932
933   private boolean eq(String s1, String s2) {
934      return Shorts.eq(! caseSensitive, s1, s2);  // NOAI
935   }
936
937   private Supplier<Object> resolver(Object input) {
938      return () -> varResolver.resolve(s(unwrap(input)));
939   }
940
941   private static Object unwrap(Object o) {
942      while (o instanceof Supplier<?> s)
943         o = s.get();
944      return o;
945   }
946}
